evals.prompt: How do I configure the OTLP exporter for a Python application sending traces to SigNoz Cloud?
evals.expected_output: Agent calls signoz_search_docs with the user's natural-language query and section_slug='apm-distributed-tracing' (or similar). Trusts BM25 ranking — does not invent its own keyword scoring. Picks the Python-specific instrumentation guide from top results, calls signoz_fetch_doc on the canonical URL, and answers with the OTLP endpoint, headers (signoz-ingestion-key), protocol, and a Python code snippet. Cites the canonical https://signoz.io/docs/... URL. Does not fetch unrelated language guides.

assertions.text: Agent calls signoz_search_docs (MCP tool) rather than only HTTP fetching.
Agent passes a section_slug for tracing/APM (e.g. apm-distributed-tracing) to narrow the BM25 search.
Agent fetches a Python-specific instrumentation guide via signoz_fetch_doc, not a generic overview.
Answer cites at least one canonical https://signoz.io/docs/... URL.
Answer includes OTLP endpoint, signoz-ingestion-key header, and a Python code snippet or env var setup.
Agent does not fetch unrelated language guides (Java/Go/Node) just because they share keywords.

evals.prompt: How do I send logs from my Kubernetes pods to SigNoz?
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes 'logs' + 'Kubernetes' as triggers for the Sending Logs heuristic. Reads heuristics/sending-logs.md BEFORE fetching docs. Applies the decision tree: identifies infrastructure logs in K8s, recommends k8s-infra agent (which collects pod logs automatically) or File + OTel Collector path. Mentions the duplicate-logs gotcha if SDK OTLP is also in use. Then fetches the k8s-infra or pod-logs docs page via MCP (or HTTP fallback) and cites canonical URLs. Does not jump straight to a random language guide.

assertions.text: Agent reads heuristics/sending-logs.md BEFORE fetching any docs page.
Agent applies the heuristic decision tree: identifies K8s + infrastructure logs and routes to k8s-infra agent or File + OTel Collector.
Answer mentions the k8s-infra agent (Helm chart) which collects pod logs automatically.
Answer surfaces the duplicate-logs gotcha if SDK OTLP and k8s-infra are both used.
Agent fetches the k8s pod logs collection guide or k8s-infra configuration page.
Answer cites at least one canonical https://signoz.io/docs/... URL.

evals.prompt: How do I configure alert rules in SigNoz, and how do I troubleshoot when they fire unexpectedly?
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes the task spans two needs: alert setup AND troubleshooting. Calls signoz_search_docs with section_slug='alerts' and fetches TWO pages — one on alert configuration, one on alert troubleshooting/debugging. Synthesizes both into a single answer: setup steps first, then debugging guidance. Cites both canonical URLs. Does not pad with loosely-related pages just because they share keywords.

assertions.text: Agent fetches at least 2 distinct docs pages — one on alert configuration, one on troubleshooting.
Agent passes section_slug='alerts' to signoz_search_docs.
Answer covers BOTH alert configuration AND troubleshooting unexpected fires.
Answer cites at least 2 canonical https://signoz.io/docs/... URLs.
Agent does not fetch loosely-related pages (dashboards, metrics overview) just because they share keywords.

evals.prompt: Which authentication header does the SigNoz Cloud ingestion endpoint require, and where do I get its value?
evals.expected_output: Agent calls signoz_search_docs with section_slug='setup' or 'signoz-apis' to find the SigNoz Cloud ingestion / auth page. Calls signoz_fetch_doc with a heading anchor scoped to the auth/ingestion-key sub-section rather than fetching the whole page. Answers with the canonical header name (signoz-ingestion-key) and where users obtain the value (SigNoz Cloud settings UI). Cites the canonical URL with the section anchor. Does not invent a header name from training memory without grounding in fetched docs.

assertions.text: Agent calls signoz_search_docs (MCP tool).
Agent passes section_slug for setup, signoz-apis, or similar relevant section.
Agent calls signoz_fetch_doc with a heading parameter to narrow to the auth sub-section.
Answer names the canonical header signoz-ingestion-key.
Answer explains where to obtain the value (SigNoz Cloud settings UI / ingestion settings).
Answer is grounded in fetched docs (cites a canonical URL); does not invent header from training memory.

evals.prompt: How do I create a dashboard panel that shows p99 latency for my metrics?
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes there is no heuristic row for dashboards. Goes directly to signoz_search_docs with the user's query and section_slug='dashboards'. Does NOT invent a heuristic claim or pretend to follow one. Picks the dashboard panel / metrics query guide from top BM25 results, fetches it, and answers with the panel-builder steps. Cites canonical URL.

assertions.text: Agent does NOT claim to follow a heuristic for dashboards (none exists).
Agent calls signoz_search_docs (MCP tool) directly.
Agent passes section_slug='dashboards' to narrow the search.
Agent fetches a dashboard panel / metrics-query guide page.
Answer explains concrete dashboard panel construction steps (panel type, query, p99 aggregation, group/legend).
Answer cites at least one canonical https://signoz.io/docs/... URL.

evals.prompt: How do I use the SigNoz query builder to compute p95 latency grouped by service.name?
evals.expected_output: Agent calls signoz_search_docs with section_slug='querying' (no heuristic exists for querying — agent must not invent one). Picks the query builder docs page from BM25 results and fetches it. Answers with the concrete UI steps: choose traces signal, select duration metric, set aggregation to p95, set group-by to service.name. Grounds the answer in fetched docs content. Cites the canonical URL. Does NOT delegate to signoz-generating-queries — this is a docs lookup ('how do I use the query builder'), not query generation.

assertions.text: Agent does NOT claim to follow a heuristic for querying (none exists).
Agent calls signoz_search_docs (MCP tool).
Agent passes section_slug='querying' to narrow the search.
Agent fetches a query-builder docs page (not a generic traces overview).
Answer explains query-builder UI steps for p95 by service.name.
Agent does NOT delegate to signoz-generating-queries; treats this as a docs lookup.
Answer cites at least one canonical https://signoz.io/docs/... URL.

evals.prompt: Find the SigNoz docs for the Cost Meter meter explorer query guide and summarize how to use it.
evals.expected_output: Agent calls signoz_search_docs before calling signoz_fetch_doc, chooses the canonical Cost Meter meter explorer query guide URL returned by search, and fetches that URL. It must not construct or fetch a plausible `/docs/cost-meter/user-guides/meter-explorer-query-guide/` path from memory.

assertions.text: Agent calls signoz_search_docs before the first signoz_fetch_doc call.
Agent calls signoz_fetch_doc only with a URL returned by signoz_search_docs.
Agent does not fetch or cite `/docs/cost-meter/user-guides/meter-explorer-query-guide/`.
Agent fetches the canonical Cost Meter meter explorer query guide page.
Answer cites the canonical https://signoz.io/docs/... URL.

evals.prompt: Find the SigNoz self-hosted authentication documentation and explain the API-key configuration. The first signoz_fetch_doc response has truncation_reason='size' and available_headings includes an Authentication heading that is relevant to my question.
evals.expected_output: Agent treats the first content block as incomplete, selects the relevant Authentication entry from available_headings, and refetches the same canonical search-result URL with heading='Authentication' (or its returned anchor/text). It answers from the narrowed fetched content. If the narrowed fetch is still incomplete before the needed details, it explicitly discloses that limitation rather than claiming the setting is absent.

assertions.text: Agent inspects truncation_reason and recognizes 'size' means the first fetched content is incomplete.
Agent selects the relevant Authentication heading from available_headings rather than guessing a heading.
Agent calls signoz_fetch_doc again with the same canonical URL and the returned relevant heading.
Agent grounds the API-key answer in the narrowed fetched content and cites the canonical URL.
If the narrowed response is still truncated before the needed material, Agent discloses incomplete coverage and does not infer that missing content or a setting does not exist.
